tools.name: news_scraper
tools.description: Scrapes recent financial news articles from various sources. Use this to understand current market sentiment, breaking news, and events that could impact bond prices.

parameters.name: keywords
parameters.description: Keywords to search for in news

parameters.name: hours_back
parameters.description: How many hours back to search

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Performs a web search to find specific, factual information or current data that is not available in other tools. Good for very specific or obscure queries.

tools.name: crisil_scraper
tools.description: Retrieves credit rating reports and rationale from CRISIL for a specific company or bond. Use this to assess credit quality, rating outlook, and default risk.

tools.name: portfolio_manager
tools.description: Accesses and manages the user's personal bond portfolio. Use this for any query that mentions 'my portfolio', 'my holdings', or requires knowledge of the user's current investments.

tools.name: bond_pricer
tools.description: Forecasts the future price of a specific bond based on various models. Use when the user asks for a price prediction.

parameters.name: days_ahead
parameters.description: Number of days ahead to forecast

tools.name: yield_forecaster
tools.description: Forecasts the entire yield curve for different tenors. Use for queries about future interest rates, yield curve shape (steepening/flattening), and macro-level rate analysis.

parameters.name: days_ahead
parameters.description: Number of days ahead to forecast

tools.name: rag_retriever
tools.description: Retrieves relevant documents and chunks from a knowledge base (RAG system). Use this for queries about RBI policies, historical analysis, or information contained in indexed documents.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Query to search in knowledge base

agents.name: query_classifier
agents.description: Analyzes and classifies the user's query to understand intent, entities, and filters. This is a good first step for most queries.

agents.name: ml_model
agents.description: Uses machine learning models to predict bond returns and price movements. This is a core agent for generating quantitative insights.

agents.name: analyst
agents.description: Performs deep financial analysis on bonds, calculating a wide range of metrics like fair value, duration, convexity, and risk scores. Essential for detailed bond evaluation.

agents.name: scoring
agents.description: Scores and ranks bonds based on a weighted combination of valuation, return, quality, and liquidity. Use this to compare multiple bonds and find the best ones.

agents.name: advisory
agents.description: Synthesizes all analysis to generate concrete, actionable trade recommendations (Buy, Sell, Hold) and a summary. This is the final step for generating user-facing advice.

agents.name: explainability
agents.description: Provides detailed explanations for recommendations, often using SHAP values or other model interpretation techniques. ONLY use this agent if the user explicitly asks for a reason or explanation (e.g., 'why?', 'explain').

planning_rules.description: Only select components absolutely necessary to answer the query

examples.query: What is the price of bond X?

planning_rules.description: If query is just asking for information (not recommendations), use ONLY tools, NO agents unless specifically needed
Only add analysis agents (ml_model, analyst, scoring, advisory) when user explicitly asks for recommendations or advice
Only use explainability agent if user explicitly asks 'why', 'explain', 'reason'
Use portfolio_manager only if query mentions 'my portfolio', 'my bonds', 'my holdings'
Use RAG retriever for queries about documents, historical analysis, RBI policies
